questions.questionText: What is the purpose of array traversal?

options.text: To visit each element in the array one by one
To delete elements from the array

questions.explanation: Array traversal means visiting each element sequentially, typically using loops to access and process each element.

questions.questionText: What is the time complexity of traversing an array with n elements?
questions.explanation: Traversing an array requires visiting each of n elements once, resulting in O(n) time complexity.

questions.questionText: Which loop is most commonly used for array traversal?
questions.explanation: The for loop with range is the most common way to traverse arrays in Python, allowing index-based access.

questions.questionText: What is Linear Search?

options.text: Searching by checking each element sequentially from start to end

questions.explanation: Linear search checks each element one by one starting from the beginning until the element is found or the array ends.

questions.questionText: What is the time complexity of Linear Search in the worst case?
questions.explanation: In the worst case, linear search must check all n elements (when the element is at the end or not present), resulting in O(n).

questions.questionText: What is Binary Search?

options.text: A search algorithm that works only on sorted arrays by dividing the search space in half
A search algorithm that checks each element one by one
A search algorithm that uses hashing
A search algorithm that requires multiple passes

questions.explanation: Binary search efficiently searches sorted arrays by repeatedly dividing the search range in half, eliminating half of remaining elements each time.

questions.questionText: What is the time complexity of Binary Search?
questions.explanation: Binary search has O(log n) time complexity because it eliminates half the search space with each comparison.

questions.questionText: Which of the following is a requirement for Binary Search?

options.text: The array must have odd number of elements
The array must be in descending order
The array size must be a power of 2

questions.explanation: Binary search requires a sorted array (ascending or descending) to work correctly, as it relies on comparison-based elimination.

questions.questionText: What is the sum of array elements [5, 10, 15, 20]?
questions.explanation: Sum = 5 + 10 + 15 + 20 = 50. Array sum is calculated by adding all elements using traversal.

questions.questionText: What is the average of array [10, 20, 30, 40]?
How many iterations are needed to traverse an array of size 5 using a for loop?

codeBlock.code:
arr = [1, 2, 3, 4, 5]
count = 0
for i in range(len(arr)):
    count += 1
# How many iterations?

questions.explanation: A for loop that traverses all elements of an array with 5 elements will iterate exactly 5 times (once per element).

questions.questionText: What is the sum of the array [2, 4, 6, 8, 10]?

codeBlock.code:
arr = [2, 4, 6, 8, 10]
total = 0
for i in range(len(arr)):
    total += arr[i]
# What is total?

questions.explanation: Sum = 2 + 4 + 6 + 8 + 10 = 30. The loop adds each element to the total.

questions.questionText: What is the average of the array [5, 15, 25, 35]?

codeBlock.code:
arr = [5, 15, 25, 35]
total = sum(arr)
average = total / len(arr)
# What is average?

questions.questionText: Using Linear Search, at what index is the element 7 found in [3, 5, 7, 9, 11]?
questions.explanation: Linear search iterates from index 0 to find 7. It's at index 2 (0-indexed).

questions.questionText: How many comparisons does Linear Search need in the worst case for an array of size 6?

codeBlock.code:
arr = [1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6]
# Linear search worst case: element not found or at end
# How many comparisons needed?

questions.explanation: In worst case, linear search checks all 6 elements, requiring 6 comparisons.

questions.questionText: Using Binary Search, how many comparisons are needed to search in a sorted array of 16 elements in the worst case?

codeBlock.code:
# Binary search worst case comparisons = log2(n) + 1
# For n = 16 elements
# How many comparisons?

questions.explanation: Binary search on 16 elements: logâ‚‚(16) + 1 = 4 + 1 = 5 comparisons at most.

questions.questionText: What is the sum of even numbers in the array [1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6]?
questions.explanation: Even numbers: 2, 4, 6. Sum = 2 + 4 + 6 = 12.

questions.questionText: Find the maximum element in [15, 7, 23, 5, 18] using traversal. What is the max?
questions.explanation: Traversing the array: 15 > 0, 7 < 15, 23 > 15, 5 < 23, 18 < 23. Maximum is 23.

questions.questionText: Count occurrences of element 5 in [5, 2, 5, 8, 5, 1] using traversal.
questions.explanation: Traversing and counting: 5 appears at indices 0, 2, and 4. Total count is 3.

questions.questionText: What is the average of positive numbers in [-5, 10, 20, -3, 15]?

codeBlock.code:
arr = [-5, 10, 20, -3, 15]
positive_sum = 0
count = 0
for i in range(len(arr)):
    if arr[i] > 0:
        positive_sum += arr[i]
        count += 1
average = positive_sum / count
# What is the average?

questions.explanation: Positive numbers: 10, 20, 15. Sum = 45. Count = 3. Average = 45/3 = 15.
